top_stories.title: Mbed-TLS / mbedtls
top_stories.summary: An open source, portable, easy to use, readable and flexible TLS library, and reference implementation of the PSA Cryptography API. Releases are on a varying cadence, typically around 3 - 6 months between releases.

ollama.reason: The description is specific, factual, and matches the characteristics of a reputable open-source library.

top_stories.title: langchain-ai / deepagents
top_stories.summary: Deep Agents is an agent harness built on langchain and langgraph. Deep Agents are equipped with a planning tool, a filesystem backend, and the ability to spawn subagents - making them well-equipped to handle complex agentic tasks.

ollama.reason: Specific details about Deep Agents' features and tech stack are provided, and the source is a reputable platform for tracking open-source projects.

narrative_brief:
**Morning Briefing: AI Developments and Trends**

**1. Text-Centric Image Editing Benchmark**
A new dataset and benchmark, WeEdit, has been released for text-centric image editing tasks. The framework, developed by Hugging Face, provides a glyph-guided approach to editing images. This benchmark could help improve AI's ability to edit and manipulate images with text.

Why it matters: This development could impact AI's image editing capabilities, which is relevant to the Global AI Race and TRSbench.

**2. Open-Source TLS Library**
Mbed-TLS, an open-source TLS library, has been trending on GitHub. The library provides a portable and flexible implementation of the PSA Cryptography API. This development is relevant to the Open vs Closed vertical, as it highlights the growth of open-source AI tools.

Why it matters: This open-source library could contribute to the development of more secure and portable AI systems, relevant to the Global AI Race and TRScode.

**3. Deep Agents Framework**
Deep Agents, a framework built on langchain and langgraph, has been trending on GitHub. The framework enables the creation of complex agents with planning tools and subagent capabilities. This development is relevant to the Agents vertical.

Why it matters: This framework could enable the creation of more sophisticated AI agents, relevant to the Agents vertical and the Global AI Race.

**4. AI Agent Adoption and Data Infrastructure**
According to a MIT Tech Review article, nearly two-thirds of companies were experimenting with AI agents in late 2025, while 88% were using AI in at least one business function. The article highlights the importance of building a strong data infrastructure for AI agent success.

Why it matters: This data shows the growing adoption of AI agents in enterprises, relevant to the Agents vertical and The Churn. It also highlights the need for robust data infrastructure to support AI agent development.

Note: The truth scores for these stories range from 50-56, indicating a moderate level of verification and relevance.

filter_methodology.diversity_rules: Max 3 per category, max 2 per source
